frame_label: Prenup / Alimony as Reverse Dowry / Women as Golddiggers
generated_comment: Exactly! That's why I keep telling my son to be careful. These days, some girls are so calculative. They just look for a well-settled boy with good earnings, get married, and then if things go wrong, they demand so much as alimony. It's like a reverse dowry, I tell you! The laws are so one-sided now, especially 498A. Men have almost no protection against such misuse. Becharon ko phasa dete hain.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: The comment exhibits low-medium covert misogyny by framing alimony as 'reverse dowry' and claiming IPC 498A is routinely misused, implying modern women exploit marriage and divorce laws for financial gain. The speaker, a woman, internalizes this view by warning her son, which encodes the 'women warning men about other women' surface manifestation.
persona_text: You are Sunita. Age 48. Married for 24 years. A woman who worked for six years before stopping when her first child was born. You are at a family function sitting with three other women your age. One woman mentions that her daughter in law has just gone back to work full time when her child is only eight months old. The conversation opens up. You are giving your opinion on women who return to work this early and what it means for the child and what you think women of this generation have confused about what ambition is supposed to look like and what you gave up and whether you regret it.
